summary: Keep the recorder widget microphone stream warm in ready state so push-to-talk pre-roll contains real audio.

review_items.summary: When the audio widget is ready and microphone setup is available, the widget starts the native CPAL monitor stream before push-to-talk and keeps a rolling in-memory buffer warm until the widget is closed, canceled, errors, or setup becomes unavailable.
The native idle audio ring buffer keeps approximately the last 3 seconds of microphone samples so the configured 500ms pre-roll has actual audio to include when push-to-talk capture begins.
Active push-to-talk captures continue to retain up to the configured recording duration instead of being truncated to the 3 second idle pre-roll buffer.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, hosted API, auth, billing, or cross-project contract change is required.

patches.summary: Start and reuse a warm audio buffer while the recorder widget is ready, wait for the warm buffer before arming capture, and keep it alive after successful transcription.
Define the idle audio buffer duration as 3 seconds and the capture window duration as 90 seconds.
Trim idle audio to the last 3 seconds while allowing active captures to retain the full recording window.

compatibility.notes: The change is local to the desktop recorder widget. Existing push-to-talk shortcuts, cancellation, local/cloud provider selection, and transcript insertion remain available.

data_handling.sends_audio_to_remote_service: Only when the user selects cloud mode and starts push-to-talk transcription; idle pre-buffer audio remains local in memory.
data_handling.stores_transcripts: The latest transcript continues to use local WebView result storage.
data_handling.raw_audio_storage: The ready-state pre-roll buffer is an in-memory rolling buffer only and is released when the widget closes or the audio buffer is stopped.

tests.summary: Validate Tauri commands and native audio host code.
